mappings.rationale: Directly covers Confucianism including its history, literature, and character as both religion and philosophy — the primary subject of 825.B.
Covers modern China including the effects of nationalism and secularism on traditional religious systems, relevant to 825.A.2.d on the modern period.
Covers modern Japan including Shinto, Buddhism, and new religious movements, relevant to 825.E on the religions of Japan.
Covers Korean history and culture including its religious traditions, directly relevant to 825.D on the religions of Korea.
Chinese literature is deeply influenced by Confucian and Taoist thought, relevant to 825.B.2 on Confucian literature and 825.C.2 on Taoist literature.
Buddhism is a major religion in East Asia that interacted deeply with Chinese, Korean, and Japanese religions, relevant to 825.A.2.c on the dominance of the Buddhist Way in China.
Tibetan Buddhism represents a distinctive East Asian Buddhist tradition that influenced Chinese religious development, relevant to 825.A.
Indian philosophical traditions influenced Chinese and Japanese religious thought through Buddhism, relevant to 825.A.2.c.
Hindu traditions influenced East Asian religions through the transmission of Buddhism, relevant to 825.A.2.c on the Buddhist Way in China.
Japanese literature is deeply influenced by Shinto and Buddhist themes, relevant to 825.E.2.c on Shinto literature and mythology.
Samurai culture was intertwined with Zen Buddhism, Confucianism, and Shinto, relevant to 825.E on the religions of Japan.
Provides comparative context for understanding East Asian religions within the broader study of religion.
The Silk Road was the conduit for the transmission of Buddhism and other religions to China, relevant to 825.A.2.c.
Covers philosophical questions relevant to understanding Confucianism and Taoism as both philosophy and religion, relevant to 825.B.3 and 825.C.3.
Covers approaches to myth relevant to understanding Chinese mythology (825.A.6) and Shinto mythology (825.E.2.c).
Covers mythological traditions including Chinese and Japanese mythology, relevant to 825.A.6 and 825.E.2.c.
